content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: OISAIR PROJECT – Two‑Way Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement
@ Document ID: contract_nli_no_align_dev_3_nda_7
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_3_nda_7

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standard library imports (implicit in most MDL environments)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
open std.collections

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Definitions
type PartyRole = Disclosing | Receiving

type Party = {
    name: string,
    role: PartyRole
}

@ Clause 4 – Representatives
type Representative = {
    party: Party,                 -- the Party to which the representative belongs
    name: string,                 -- personal name of the representative
    is_professional_advisor: bool -- true for consultants, agents, advisors, etc.
}

@ Clause 1 – Confidential Information (simplified)
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    field: string,   -- e.g. “technical data”, “business plan”, …
    content: string  -- opaque payload; not inspected by the solver
}

@ Clause 5 – Disclosure event (the act of sharing information)
type Disclosure = {
    from: Party,                     -- the disclosing side (always the Disclosing Party)
    to: Party,                       -- the recipient side (Receiving Party or a third party)
    info: ConfidentialInformation,
    via: Option<Representative>,     -- present when the receipt is a Representative of the Receiving Party
    date: int                        -- abstract day count from Effective Date (used for temporal constraints)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the concrete objects that the solver will assign values to
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties (Clause 1)
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ Representative list (Clause 4)
entity representative_list: List<Representative>

@ Confidential information (Clause 1)
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInformation

@ Disclosure record (Clause 4 & 5)
entity disclosure: Disclosure

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper Functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 4 – a party is a Representative of the Receiving Party if it appears in the list
func is_representative(rec: Representative, rec_list: List<Representative>) -> bool:
    let rec_name = rec.name
    case rec_list:
        | Empty(_): false
        | Cons(head, tail):
            (head.name = rec_name) or is_representative(rec, tail)

@ Clause 4 – a Disclosure is to a Representative when the `via` field is present
func disclosure_is_to_representative(d: Disclosure) -> bool:
    case d.via:
        | None(_): false
        | Some(rep): true

@ Clause 5 – a Disclosure is to a third‑party (non‑Representative) when `via` is None
func disclosure_is_to_third_party(d: Disclosure) -> bool:
    not disclosure_is_to_representative(d)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – normative requirements (deontic modalities + temporal operators)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 3 – Obligation: the Receiving Party must keep all Confidential Information secret
rule O keep_confidentiality:
    -- the rule applies to every Disclosure the the Receiving Party is involved in
    (disclosure.from = disclosing_party) and
    (disclosure.to = receiving_party) and
    (disclosure.info = confidential_info) implies
    (disclosure_is_to_representative(disclosure) or disclosure_is_to_third_party(disclosure) = false) always

@ Clause 4 – Permission: Disclosure to a Representative is allowed
rule P allow_disclosure_to_representative:
    disclosure_is_to_representative(disclosure) always

@ Clause 4 – Prohibition: Disclosure to a non‑Representative third party is forbidden
rule F forbid_disclosure_to_third_party:
    not disclosure_is_to_third_party(disclosure) always

@ Clause 5 – Obligation: the Receiving Party must take reasonable measures to protect the information
rule O reasonable_measures:
    -- a Boolean field could be added to the Receiving Party; here we model it as a fact that must hold
    receiving_party.role = Receiving implies true always

@ Clause 10 – Obligation (post‑termination): return or destroy all Confidential Information within 7 days
rule O return_or_destroy_after_termination:
    -- `date` is the day count from the Effective Date; Termination Date = 5*365 days
    let termination_day = 5 * 365
    (disclosure.date > termination_day) implies
    (disclosure.date <= termination_day + 7) always

@ Clause 16 – Prohibition: the existence of the Agreement may not be disclosed
rule F forbid_agreement_disclosure:
    -- we model the Agreement itself as an entity `agreement_exists`; any attempt to disclose it is prohibited
    true always   -- placeholder – actual detection would require an additional entity; omitted for brevity

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of module
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
